behavior_in_task_description.reason: instruction.md names the file packages/opencode/src/skill/index.ts, the helpers add/scan/loadSkills, Config.Service, Bus.Service, defaultLayer, Effect.runPromise, Effect.promise, .then(), Promise.all — all strings tested.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what's broken and what patterns to use (Effect.gen/fn/fnUntraced, yield Config.Service) but doesn't provide exact code; agent must still design the refactor.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests accept any of Effect.fn/fnUntraced/gen, and check for presence of Config.Service/Bus.Service yields or layer type — reasonable alternative correct fixes would pass.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: All tests are regex/string checks on skill/index.ts source (via _get_analysis and _read_stripped); no import/execution of the code. E.g., test_loadskills_is_effect_generator only regex-matches 'loadSkills = Effect.(fnUntraced|fn|gen)'.

test_not_tautological.reason: f2p tests require both removal of async patterns and presence of Effect generators plus yielded services; a stub can't satisfy these conjunctions.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Each instruction bullet (async removal, Config.get removal, Effect.runPromise removal, monolithic promise wrapper, .then, Promise.all, layer deps, defaultLayer provides) has a corresponding test.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with section headers (Fail-to-pass pr_diff, Pass-to-pass pr_diff, agent_config) and annotated comments like '[pr_diff] fail_to_pass'.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is a code refactor, no structured data output produced.

no_typos.reason: Paths like packages/opencode/src/skill/index.ts and identifiers (Effect.fnUntraced, Config.Service) are consistent throughout.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names the file under audit which is appropriate since the task is specifically about refactoring that service; no exact line/patch provided.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: Code-only task, no agent config edits.
